title: Step Into Another World: How VR Is Changing Our Daily Lives
title_zh: 步入另一个世界：虚拟现实如何改变我们的日常生活
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，探讨虚拟现实技术在医疗、教育、娱乐和职场等领域的实际应用，展示其如何重塑我们的生活方式。

paragraphs.en: Imagine putting on a headset and instantly finding yourself on the top of Mount Everest, inside a human heart, or at a concert thousands of miles away. This is not science fiction — it is <span>virtual</span> reality (VR). Over the past decade, VR has moved from <span>gaming</span> to fields like medicine, education, and business. Today, it is <span>transforming</span> the way we learn, heal, and connect with others.
paragraphs.zh: 想象一下，戴上头盔，瞬间发现自己站在珠穆朗玛峰之巅、一颗人类心脏内部，或者千里之外的演唱会现场。这不是科幻——这是虚拟现实（VR）。过去十年间，VR已从游戏领域扩展到医学、教育和商业等领域。如今，它正在改变我们学习、治疗和与他人联系的方式。

paragraphs.en: One of the most promising <span>applications</span> of VR is in healthcare. Surgeons can practice complex operations in a risk-free <span>environment</span>. For example, at Stanford University, medical students use VR to simulate heart surgery. This hands-on training improves their skills without endangering real patients. Moreover, VR is used to treat <span>phobias</span> like fear of heights or flying. Patients are gradually exposed to their fears in a controlled virtual setting, which helps them overcome anxiety.
paragraphs.zh: VR最有前景的应用之一是在医疗保健领域。外科医生可以在无风险的环境中练习复杂的手术。例如，在斯坦福大学，医学生使用VR模拟心脏手术。这种实操训练提高了他们的技能，且不会危及真实患者。此外，VR还被用于治疗恐惧症，如恐高或飞行恐惧。患者在一个受控的虚拟环境中逐渐暴露于恐惧中，这有助于他们克服焦虑。

paragraphs.en: Education is another area <span>benefiting</span> greatly from VR. Instead of just reading about ancient Rome, students can walk through the Colosseum in a virtual tour. History lessons become <span>immersive</span> experiences that make learning unforgettable. A study by the University of Maryland found that students who used VR retained information better than those who studied with traditional methods. VR also allows <span>remote</span> learners to attend virtual classrooms, bridging the gap between physical and digital education.
paragraphs.zh: 教育是另一个从VR中大大受益的领域。学生不只是阅读关于古罗马的内容，而是可以在虚拟游览中走过罗马斗兽场。历史课变成了沉浸式体验，使学习令人难忘。马里兰大学的一项研究发现，使用VR的学生比用传统方法学习的学生记忆信息更好。VR还允许远程学习者进入虚拟教室，缩小了线下教育与数字教育之间的差距。

paragraphs.en: The entertainment <span>industry</span> has also embraced VR. Beyond video games, VR cinemas and concerts offer audiences a 360-degree view. For instance, the band Coldplay once held a VR concert that attracted 18 million viewers worldwide. People can watch from the front row without leaving their homes. This <span>technology</span> is making entertainment more <span>accessible</span> and interactive than ever before.
paragraphs.zh: 娱乐产业也拥抱了VR。除了电子游戏，VR影院和音乐会为观众提供了360度视角。例如，酷玩乐队曾举办一场VR演唱会，吸引了全球1800万观众。人们足不出户就能从前排观看。这项技术正使娱乐变得比以往任何时候都更易获取且更具互动性。

paragraphs.en: In the workplace, VR is used for training and <span>collaboration</span>. Companies like Walmart have trained over one million employees using VR simulations for customer service and safety. Architects and engineers can walk through <span>virtual</span> building models before construction begins, <span>identifying</span> problems early. This saves time and money.
paragraphs.zh: 在工作场所，VR被用于培训和协作。像沃尔玛这样的公司已使用VR模拟培训超过一百万名员工，用于客户服务和安全培训。建筑师和工程师可以在施工开始前在虚拟建筑模型中行走，及早发现问题。这节省了时间和金钱。

paragraphs.en: Despite its benefits, VR still faces <span>challenges</span>. High-quality headsets are expensive, and some users experience <span>motion</span> sickness after prolonged use. Additionally, there are concerns about privacy: VR devices collect data about users' movements and <span>environment</span>. However, as technology improves and costs drop, these issues are likely to be <span>addressed</span>.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管有这些好处，VR仍面临挑战。高质量头显价格昂贵，一些用户在长时间使用后会感到晕动症。此外，还有隐私方面的担忧：VR设备收集有关用户动作和环境的数据。然而，随着技术改进和成本下降，这些问题很可能会得到解决。

paragraphs.en: The future of VR looks <span>bright</span>. Researchers are developing lighter headsets and more realistic <span>graphics</span>. Some experts predict that within a decade, VR will be as common as smartphones. As the line between the real and the virtual continues to <span>blur</span>, we must learn to use this powerful tool wisely.
paragraphs.zh: VR的未来看起来很光明。研究人员正在开发更轻的头显和更逼真的图像。一些专家预测，十年内，VR将像智能手机一样普遍。随着现实与虚拟之间的界限继续模糊，我们必须学会明智地使用这一强大工具。

paragraphs.en: In conclusion, VR is not just a <span>trend</span>; it is a <span>transformative</span> technology that is already <span>affecting</span> many aspects of our lives. Whether it's learning a new skill, overcoming a fear, or exploring distant places, VR offers experiences that were once unimaginable. The key is to embrace its potential while remaining aware of its limitations.
paragraphs.zh: 总之，VR不仅仅是一种趋势；它是一种变革性的技术，已经影响到我们生活的许多方面。无论是学习新技能、克服恐惧，还是探索遥远的地方，VR都提供了曾经无法想象的体验。关键是在拥抱其潜力的同时，保持对其局限性的认识。

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: VR is mainly used for entertainment and gaming.
VR has a wide range of practical applications in various fields.
VR is too expensive to be widely adopted.
VR will replace smartphones in the future.
questions.explain: 主旨题。文章从医疗、教育、娱乐、职场等多个方面介绍了VR的应用，因此B选项“VR在各个领域有广泛的实际应用”是正确概括。A过于片面；C是挑战之一但非主旨；D是预测但非主旨。

questions.q: According to the passage, how does VR help people with phobias?
options: By forcing them to face real fears immediately.
By gradually exposing them to fears in a controlled setting.
By providing medication during VR sessions.
questions.explain: 细节题。文章第二段提到VR用于治疗恐惧症时，“患者在一个受控的虚拟环境中逐渐暴露于恐惧中”，对应B选项。A中的“immediately”错误；C和D未提及。

questions.q: What challenge does VR currently face according to the passage?
questions.explain: 细节题。第六段提到“高质量头显昂贵”和“晕动症”，对应B选项。A、C、D与原文不符或未提及。

questions.q: The word 'immersive' in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:
questions.explain: 词义题。第三段描述历史课变成immersive experiences，结合语境指“沉浸式的、使人全神贯注的”，因此engrossing（引人入胜的）最接近。expensive（昂贵的）不符合；educational（教育的）虽有关联但不够准确；virtual（虚拟的）是VR的特征但非该词本意。
